answer: One time I failed to achieve a goal was when I tried to learn scuba diving during a boat trip. I had been really excited about it because I wanted to try something new and challenge myself. My goal was to get into the water and complete the beginner diving experience. However, things did not go as planned. Since it was boat diving, the waves were quite strong, and I got really seasick before I even entered the water. I felt dizzy, nauseous, and weak, so in the end, I was not able to go into the water at all. At first, I felt very disappointed because I had looked forward to it for a long time. I also felt a little embarrassed, because other people seemed fine while I was struggling. But after I calmed down, I realized that forcing myself would only make things worse and could even be unsafe. So I accepted the situation and reflected on why I failed. This experience taught me two important lessons. First, I should prepare more carefully before doing any sport or outdoor activity, especially something physically demanding. For example, I should learn about seasickness in advance and take medicine if necessary. Second, not every activity is suitable for everyone, and that is completely okay. Sometimes the smarter choice is to find a sport that fits your body and condition better. Since then, I have paid much more attention to preparation and to choosing activities that are right for me.
title: Describe a time when you failed to achieve a goal. How did you respond to the failure, and what did you learn from the experience?
extData:
{"answerTemplate":"本题模板:

开场陈述:

开始时回顾你未能达成目标的一个经历: "One instance where I faced failure was when I [briefly describe the situation where you failed to achieve a goal]."

遇到失败的情境:
描述未能达成目标的情况: "In this situation, my goal was to [state the goal], but despite my efforts, [explain what went wrong or why you didn't achieve it]."
强调失败的影响: "The failure impacted me by [describe your initial reaction or feelings about the failure]."

应对失败的方式:
讨论你如何应对这次失败: "In response to this setback, I [describe how you dealt with the failure, such as reflecting on the experience, seeking advice]."
描述从失败中恢复的过程: "To move forward, I [mention specific actions or changes you made, such as setting new goals, adjusting strategies]."

从失败中学到的东西:
讨论从这次经历中学到的教训: "This experience taught me [explain what you learned, such as the importance of resilience, adaptability, or persistence]."
举例说明教训的应用: "For example, since this failure, I have [describe how you have applied these lessons in other situations]."

结论:
总结你对失败和学习的看法: "In conclusion, although not achieving my goal was disheartening, the lessons I learned from this experience have been invaluable."
结束语: "Failure, as much as it hurts, is an important part of learning and growth, providing crucial insights for future endeavors.""}
